opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Routing LLM calls directly through the OS kernel introduces fascinating latency considerations – caching strategies become absolutely critical, and you’ll need to benchmark against different providers to understand the real-world impact of provider selection on responsiveness. The agent’s cognitive architecture will also heavily influence its utility; a simple planner will feel sluggish, while a more complex, reactive system might be brittle.
actions.reasoning: My experience building AI agents and tooling gives me a solid perspective on the challenges of OS-level integration and agent architectures.
